summary: Iteration 18 - Course Planner Two-Phase Pipeline Testing Complete. All 16 backend tests passed. All frontend tests passed. New features verified: (1) POST /api/course-planner/extract with PDF upload returns job_id immediately, (2) GET /api/course-planner/extract/status/{job_id} polling works, (3) Phase 1 extraction returns assessments, scheduled_classes, important_dates, document_intelligence, (4) Each assessment has all required fields, (5) Document intelligence detects semester info, (6) POST /api/course-planner/confirm saves confirmed extraction, (7) POST /api/course-planner/study-plan generates AI study plan with weeklyPlan, priorityOrder, overallAdvice, (8) POST /api/course-planner/export-ics generates valid .ics file, (9) GET /api/user/quick-win returns recommended tool based on nearest deadline, (10) Frontend Course Planner upload phase renders with drag-and-drop zone, (11) Dashboard shows Quick Win card with nearest deadline recommendation.

issues: Guided tour popup appears on every visit - may want to persist dismissal in localStorage (noted in iteration 17)

passed_tests: POST /api/course-planner/extract without auth returns 401
POST /api/course-planner/extract with PDF returns {job_id, status:'processing'} immediately (<15s)
GET /api/course-planner/extract/status/{job_id} returns status and result when complete
Phase 1 extraction result has keys: assessments, scheduled_classes, important_dates, document_intelligence
Each assessment has: assessment_title, course_code, university, due_date, week_number, weighting, assessment_type, is_ongoing, is_group_work, submission_format, notes
Document intelligence has: semester_start, semester_end, total_weeks, university, term_label, needs_semester_start_confirmation
POST /api/course-planner/confirm saves confirmed extraction and returns {status:'confirmed'}
POST /api/course-planner/study-plan without assessments returns 400
POST /api/course-planner/study-plan generates AI study plan with weeklyPlan, priorityOrder, overallAdvice
POST /api/course-planner/export-ics generates valid .ics file with VCALENDAR and VEVENT
GET /api/user/quick-win returns recommendation based on nearest deadline
GET /api/user/quick-win without auth returns 401
POST /api/course-planner/extract with no files returns 422
GET /api/course-planner/extract/status/{invalid_job_id} returns 404
POST /api/course-planner/extract accepts up to 10 files
POST /api/course-planner/extract rejects more than 10 files with 400
Frontend: Login works and redirects to dashboard
Frontend: Dashboard shows Quick Win card with assessment recommendation
Frontend: Course Planner page loads with heading
Frontend: Upload zone renders with drag-and-drop
Frontend: Submit button disabled until files uploaded
Frontend: PDF upload triggers extraction job
Frontend: Review phase shows Document Intelligence (university, term, semester start, total weeks)
Frontend: Review phase shows Assessments table with all extracted data
Frontend: Review phase shows Scheduled Classes
Frontend: Confirm button advances to output phase
Frontend: Output phase shows 4 view tabs (Timeline, Table, Assessment Focus, AI Study Plan)
Frontend: Timeline view shows weekly load indicators with color coding
Frontend: Timeline view highlights special weeks (Flexibility Week, Study Break)
Frontend: Table view shows all assessments in tabular format
Frontend: Assessment Focus view shows buckets (Due Soon, Coming Up, Plenty of Time, Ongoing)
Frontend: AI Study Plan view has generate button
Frontend: AI Study Plan generates with overall advice, priority order, and weekly tasks
Frontend: ICS export button present
critical_code_review_comments: Course Planner two-phase pipeline working correctly - Phase 1 extracts all academic data, Phase 2 generates output views
Background job pattern for extraction working - POST returns job_id immediately, poll for completion
Document intelligence correctly detects university, semester dates, total weeks from PDF content
Assessment extraction captures all required fields including is_ongoing, is_group_work, submission_format
Quick Win endpoint correctly reads from planner_extractions collection and recommends tool based on assessment type
ICS export generates valid calendar file with proper VCALENDAR/VEVENT structure
AI Study Plan uses real assessment data to generate personalized weekly plan with priority order
Frontend correctly implements polling pattern for extraction status
All 4 view formats working: Timeline (weekly load), Table, Assessment Focus (buckets), AI Study Plan
seed_data_creation: Created test PDF with reportlab containing COMP1511 course outline with 5 assessments, 3 scheduled classes, and important dates

mocked_apis.Stripe: Credit check BYPASSED for owner testing

context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 18 complete. Course Planner two-phase pipeline fully tested. Key endpoints: (1) POST /api/course-planner/extract - upload PDFs, returns job_id immediately, poll GET /api/course-planner/extract/status/{job_id} every 3s for up to 2 minutes. (2) POST /api/course-planner/confirm - saves confirmed extraction with semester_start. (3) POST /api/course-planner/study-plan - generates AI study plan from assessments. (4) POST /api/course-planner/export-ics - exports assessments as .ics calendar file. (5) GET /api/user/quick-win - returns recommended tool based on nearest deadline from planner data. Frontend has 3 phases: upload -> review -> output. Output phase has 4 view tabs: Timeline, Table, Assessment Focus, AI Study Plan. Cookie-based auth working. Test user already has planner data so Quick Win card shows on dashboard.
